subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 1.1. DESIGN IN NATURE AND MANMADE ENVIRONMENT
content_standard: B7 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of design as a concept in relation to the elements and principles of design and as a medium for creative expression of design in nature and the manmade environment.
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of design as a concept and its importance and role as a medium for creative expression of design in nature and the manmade environment.
examplars:
1. Research to determine and record the meaning, relevance and role of design in nature and the manmade environment for reflection and discussion.
2. Identify and reflect on selected natural and manmade designs to appreciate and determine how nature has influenced manmade designs to benefit society for appreciation and discussion.
3. Compare and contrast selected natural and manmade designs to determine and record their similarities and differences for reflection and inspiration to create and illustrate own design ideas (e.g. rivers/roads, anthills/houses, etc.)

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 1.1. DESIGN IN NATURE AND MANMADE ENVIRONMENT
content_standard: B7 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of design as a concept in relation to the elements and principles of design and as a medium for creative expression of design in nature and the manmade environment.
indicator_description: Research to identify and record what constitutes the 'elements of design' in nature and as building blocks for creative expression of design ideas
examplars:
1. Research to deduce and record what constitutes the 'elements of design' and their application as building blocks for creative expression of design for reflection and discussion.
2. Explore the natural and manmade environments to identify and document examples of elements of design for reflection and discussion.
3. Reflect on knowledge gained from discussions to create patterns that reflect selected elements of design using available manual and digital tools, materials and techniques for display and sharing.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 1.1. DESIGN IN NATURE AND MANMADE ENVIRONMENT
content_standard: B7 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of design as a concept in relation to the elements and principles of design and as a medium for creative expression of design in nature and the manmade environment.
indicator_description: Research to identify and record in writing what constitutes the principles of design and describe how they are used to organise the elements of design into building blocks for creative expression of design in nature and the manmade environment
examplars:
1. Search for and organise relevant information to describe and record the principles of design and how they apply in nature and the manmade environment for reflection.
2. Identify, describe and record examples of principles of design in nature and the manmade environment for appreciation and discussion.
3. Apply knowledge gained to select examples of elements and principles of design to create own designs using available manual and digital tools, materials and techniques for appreciation, display and feedback.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate understanding of relevant Visual Arts media and techniques and their application to still-life drawing, shading, pattern making and modelling
indicator_description: Identify and determine the nature and uses of tools, materials and techniques needed for still-life drawing and shading, pattern making and modelling
examplars:
1. Identify, describe and record relevant tools, materials and techniques for still-life drawing and shading, pattern making and modelling from a variety of sources.
2. Test and classify tools, materials and techniques for still-life drawing and shading, pattern making and modelling according to their nature and use.
3. Create patterns and moulds with selected elements of design for still-life drawing, pattern making and modelling.
4. Select the most appropriate tools, materials and techniques and apply them in a safe way, to create still-life drawings, patterns and models for appreciation.
5. Apply knowledge and skills in cleaning-up, maintaining and storing of tools, materials and equipment in a sustainable manner.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate understanding of relevant Visual Arts media and techniques and their application to still-life drawing, shading, pattern making and modelling
indicator_description: Apply techniques of still-life drawing and shading to make own visual artworks
examplars:
1. Identify and use the techniques in still-life drawing and shading to create own artworks.
2. Use various shading techniques in still-life drawing to create own artworks.
3. Display artworks for appreciation and reflection and use appropriate language to give supportive and informative peer and self-evaluation.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate understanding of relevant Visual Arts media and techniques and their application to still-life drawing, shading, pattern making and modelling
indicator_description: Experiment by using the techniques to test and prepare clay, pulp paper (papier-mâché) for modelling
examplars:
1.Prepare and test clay and pulp paper for modelling
2. Identify and use hand building techniques for modelling such as coiling, slab building, pinch pot, modelling, indenting, relief in clay, play dough, plasticine, papier-mâché (pulp paper).
3. Display models for appreciation and reflection and use appropriate language to give supportive and informative peer and self-evaluation.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate understanding of relevant Visual Arts media and techniques and their application to still-life drawing, shading, pattern making and modelling
indicator_description: Apply the techniques in pattern making using available media to create visual artworks
examplars:
1. Identify and describe the techniques and media for pattern making.
2. Apply relevant pattern making techniques with available media to create own visual artworks.
3. Display patterns for appreciation and reflection and use appropriate language to give supportive and informative peer and self-evaluation.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate understanding and apply scale, note durational values and simple time beat patterns in music
indicator_description: Sing in pitch the diatonic major scale when playing the John Curwen's Hand Sign Game by Lahingor using solfege
examplars:
1. Sing in pitch one octave ascending and descending from doh and/or any other scale degree
2. Match the seven alphabets to the notes in the C major scale in the treble clef by placing them under their locations (i.e., on line or in space).

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate understanding and apply scale, note durational values and simple time beat patterns in music
indicator_description: Identify durational symbols and move/perform their value in relation to the semibreve
examplars: 1. Internalise the arithmetic or numeral value of note durations against the crochet () and perform by clapping. E.g. clapping the following lines individually or in groups:

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate understanding and apply scale, note durational values and simple time beat patterns in music
indicator_description: Conduct songs in simple duple, triple and quadruple time
examplars:
1. Explain the concept of time signature in simple time in music.
2. Rehearse and conduct a group by using simple time beat patterns and use peer- and self-evaluation to review work.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate understanding and apply media, voice and movement techniques in dance and drama
indicator_description: Identify and demonstrate the various Ghanaian dance/body movements, positions and voice projection patterns
examplars:
1. Identify and perform Ghanaian physical exercises, rhythmic/theatre games, simple dance movements, etc.
2. Examine, record and reflect on different types of voices in speech and song.
3. Use body posture and voice quality as instruments of performance in Ghanaian dance and drama.
4. Identify and demonstrate various facial expressions and gestures associated with Ghanaian dancing and acting and use peer- and self-evaluation.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.1.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate understanding and apply media, voice and movement techniques in dance and drama
indicator_description: Explore and experiment with techniques of dance, body movements and voice projections in the Ghanaian performance space
examplars:
1. Identify and explain the basic parts of the performance space, body profiles and positions.
2. Mimicking and characterisation of different postures and make facial expressions of characters in the performance space.
3. Demonstrate low/medium/high levels of indigenous genre dance movements.
4. Demonstrate flexibility in body movements in the performance space and use peer- and self-evaluation.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to produce, display and appraise own creative artworks that reflect in the range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Design and produce own visual artworks that reflect the history and culture of the people of the local community
examplars:
1. Gather and record relevant information for developing ideas to design own visual artworks that reflect the history and culture of the people of the local community.
2. Apply recorded ideas and design thinking process to create 2D and 3D artworks to address social, cultural and historical problems in the community such as artworks of painting, landscape, poster design, repeat pattern or sculpture.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to produce, display and appraise own creative artworks that reflect in the range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Plan a display of own and others' artworks that reflect the history and culture of the people in the community
examplars:
1. Demonstrate understanding and skill in keeping portfolio of artworks.
2. Apply understanding and skills in planning, displaying and exhibiting artworks in class, school and the community to reflect the history and culture of the people in the community.
3. Reflect and use comments to refine own and others' artworks and the exhibition.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to produce, display and appraise own creative artworks that reflect in the range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Organise an appreciation and appraisal of own and others artworks that reflect the history and culture of the people in the community
examplars:
1. Prepare criteria to appreciate and appraise own and others' artworks using relevant art vocabulary and write report on own aesthetic experience.
2. Reflect and share aesthetic experience for future refinement and modification.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.2 Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to create and display own creative musical art works that reflect the range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Create and produce own musical art works that reflect the history and culture of the people of the community
examplars:
1. Research, select and record indigenous musical genre of the community that expresses personal experiences and interests, moods, visual images, concepts, texts, or storylines.
2. Create rehearsal plan for performing one of the selected works in Exemplar I by identifying and allocating time to the various aspects - singing, drumming, dancing, costume, venue, etc.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.2 Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to create and display own creative musical art works that reflect the range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Plan a display of own and others' musical works that reflect the history and culture of the people in the community
examplars: 1. Give a class concert (to be video recorded) that begins with a presentation on the style, historical and cultural context of the compositions selected in B7. 2.2.2.4. Exemplar 1.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.2 Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to create and display own creative musical art works that reflect the range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Organise an appreciation and appraisal of own and others' musical works that reflect the history and culture of the people in the community
examplars:
1. Conduct an enquiry by playing the recorded video clip from B7. 2.2.2.4 Exemplar I to a group of people (including learners, staff and parents) to collect data and write an aesthetic appreciation report.
2. Reflect and refine own work using the feedback from the appraisal and aesthetic appreciation report.
3. Disseminate the findings of your research to the class by giving a presentation.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.3 Dance and Drama: Demonstrate the ability to use concepts of design process (idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect in the range of different times and cultures in dance and drama.
indicator_description: Design and produce own artworks in dance and drama that reflect the history and culture of the people in the community
examplars:
1. Discuss an artist and his works in your community.
2. Design and produce own dance piece and drama skit.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.3 Dance and Drama: Demonstrate the ability to use concepts of design process (idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect in the range of different times and cultures in dance and drama.
indicator_description: Plan a display of own and others' artwork in dance and drama that reflect the history and culture of the community
examplars:
1. Describe how to plan a dance piece and drama skit for a performance.
2. Choose an appropriate venue to showcase the performance and spread the information about it.
3. Rehearse, perform and record the planned dance piece and drama skit.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B7.2.2.3 Dance and Drama: Demonstrate the ability to use concepts of design process (idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect in the range of different times and cultures in dance and drama.
indicator_description: Organise an appreciation and appraisal of own and others' artworks in dance and drama that reflect the history and culture of the people of the community
examplars:
1. Invite an audience to watch the performance of own and others for appreciation and appraisal.
2. Note and write the strengths and weaknesses of the production and performance for discussion and modification of future performances.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from visual artworks in the community that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Narrate own views of the history, culture, environment and topical issues in the community
examplars:
1. Identify and use a range of techniques such as observational drawings, sketches, own photographs and material such as adverts and images from the internet or from printed media, to record own views on the history, culture, environment and topical issues in the community.
2. Discuss and make individual or collaborative presentations on information recorded.
3. Review and reflect on presentations to build consensus on the history, culture, environment and topical issues in the community.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from visual artworks in the community that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Identify visual artworks that reflect the history, culture, environment and topical issues in the community
examplars:
1. Scout for and record visual artworks found in the community by taking photographs and making drawings from direct observation where possible.
2. Discuss, classify and group visual artworks found in the community according to their nature.
3. Discuss in groups to appraise how the history, culture, environment and topical issues in the community are reflected in the selected visual artworks and document your findings using art specific language such as content, colour scheme, symbolism, cultural significance.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B7. 2.3.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from indigenous creative musical forms and art musicians in the immediate community that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Identify indigenous and art musicians in the community whose works reflect the history, culture, environment and topical issues
examplars:
1. Select one indigenous or popular musician and assess their contribution to the society.
2. Transcribe any song in the community that promotes and sensitises the public on emerging topical issue.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B7. 2.3.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from indigenous creative musical forms and art musicians in the immediate community that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Appreciate and appraise an indigenous and a neo-traditional group within the community based on their style, instruments, song themes, dance movements, etc.
examplars:
1. Research, select and document an indigenous and a neo-traditional group within the community based on their style, instruments, song themes, dance movements, etc.
2. Compare and contrast the two selected indigenous and neo-traditional types.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B7.2.3.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from creative artworks of dancers and actors in the community that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues.
indicator_description: Select artworks of dance and drama artistes in the community or other places, and identify the history, culture, environment and topical issues that are reflected in them
examplars:
1. Research and write about the history, culture and important sites of the environment.
2. Select a dance or play and identify and write about the history, culture, environment and topical issues in them.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B7.2.3.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from creative artworks of dancers and actors in the community that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues.
indicator_description: Analyse works of other dance and drama artistes based on the history, culture, environment and topical issues in and around their communities
examplars:
1. Watch video clips of other artistes that reflect the historical, cultural and topical issues in dance and drama.
2. Analyse the video clips watched and document the historical, cultural and topical issues that are reflected in them.
3. Reflect on and discuss to generate ideas from the video clips watched.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B7.2.3.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from creative artworks of dancers and actors in the community that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues.
indicator_description: Organise a group discussion to appreciate dance and/or plays of own and other cultures, environment and topical issues.
examplars: 1. View and analyse a dance and/or drama performance from own or other cultures based on environment and topical issues for documentation.
